zotero.abstractNote: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance on a wide range of natural language processing tasks. However, it remains unclear whether they can perform causal reasoning, a fundamental aspect of human intelligence. In this paper, we investigate whether LLMs can distinguish cause from effect. We propose a set of causal reasoning tasks and evaluate several state-of-the-art LLMs on these tasks. Our results show that while LLMs can perform well on some tasks, they struggle with others, suggesting that they do not yet possess a robust understanding of causality.
